And part of what that's calling out is most of the LLMs can't read JavaScript.
Gemini is the only exception.
So if you do have, what it's saying is we identify that there is JavaScript on there.
And so what we're calling out for you is if you do have a bunch of JavaScript, that content is not getting picked up, indexed by the LLMs.
And so if you have that, what we recommend, and there is a Help Center article that talks about all these things as well.
But if you have a lot of JavaScript, what we recommend is then replicate the content in HTML on the web page.
Don't change anything to your human-facing version, but just on the web page.
Replicate in HTML the content that's being created and being surfaced up through JavaScript so that that content is also flowing over when your site gets indexed.
Okay.
And...

The strategy that I recommend is if you're using our AI traffic tab, yeah, if you're using that, because that syncs up with your Google Analytics, they're kind of in the bottom right where it says your AI landing pages.

Wherever you're getting the most traffic, I always like to go optimize for that and say, okay, I want to make sure not only if we're getting a lot of traffic, I want to make sure we are surfacing up all the content that we want.

This is a core element of our offering, of our product, of our brand, whatever.
